300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits
396	
397	
398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results
477	
478	
479	def _random_sample(
480	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
481	    random_samples: torch.Tensor,
482	) -> SampleResultType:
483	    """Run random sampling on a given samples.
484	
485	    Args:
486	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
487	        random_samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The
488	            length of samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
489	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
490	    Returns:
491	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
492	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
493	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
494	    """
495	    # Find the maximum n value of the prompt phase requests.
496	    random_samples = random_samples.cpu()
497	    sample_idx = 0
498	    results: SampleResultType = []
499	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
500	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
501	            results.append(([], []))
502	            continue
503	
504	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
505	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
506	        is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
507	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
508	        if is_prompt:
509	            # Prompt phase.
510	            parent_ids = [0] * sampling_params.n
511	            next_token_ids = random_samples[
512	                sample_idx, :sampling_params.n].tolist()
513	        else:
514	            # Generation phase.
515	            parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
516	            next_token_ids = random_samples[sample_idx:sample_idx +
517	                                            num_parent_seqs, 0].tolist()
518	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
519	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
520	    return results
521	
522	
523	# torch.multinomial forces a GPU<->CPU sync.
524	# Therefore, we use an optimized implementation instead.
525	# Note that we always sample with replacement.
526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)
548	
549	
550	def _top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer(
551	        probs: torch.Tensor, top_ks: torch.Tensor, top_ps: torch.Tensor,
552	        num_samples: int, seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]]):
553	    if num_samples > 1:
554	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
555	        top_ks = top_ks.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
556	        top_ps = top_ps.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
557	    batch_next_token_ids = flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling(
558	        probs,
559	        top_ks,
560	        top_ps,
561	    )
562	    return batch_next_token_ids.view(-1, num_samples)

593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue

614	def _sample_with_torch(
615	    probs: torch.Tensor,
616	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
617	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
618	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
619	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
620	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
621	) -> SampleReturnType:
622	    '''Torch-oriented _sample() implementation.
623	
624	    Single-step scheduling:
625	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
626	    * Immediately Pythonize sampling result
627	
628	    Multi-step scheduling:
629	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
630	    * Defer Pythonization & preserve GPU-side
631	      tensors required for Pythonization
632	    '''
633	
634	    categorized_seq_group_ids: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
635	        t: []
636	        for t in SamplingType
637	    }
638	    categorized_sample_indices = sampling_metadata.categorized_sample_indices
639	    for i, seq_group in enumerate(sampling_metadata.seq_groups):
640	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
641	        sampling_type = sampling_params.sampling_type
642	        categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type].append(i)
643	
644	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType = {}
645	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType = {}
646	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType = {}
647	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
648	
649	    # Create output tensor for sampled token ids.
650	    if include_gpu_probs_tensor:
651	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = torch.full((logprobs.shape[0], 1),
652	                                              VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
653	                                              dtype=torch.long,
654	                                              device=logprobs.device)
655	    else:
656	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = None
657	
658	    # Counterintiutively, having two loops here is actually faster.
659	    # The first loop can run without waiting on GPU<->CPU sync.
660	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
661	        sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices[sampling_type]
662	        num_tokens = len(sample_indices)
663	        if num_tokens == 0:
664	            continue
665	
666	        seq_group_id = categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type]
667	        seq_groups = [sampling_metadata.seq_groups[i] for i in seq_group_id]
668	        sample_metadata[sampling_type] = (seq_group_id, seq_groups)
669	        long_sample_indices = sample_indices.long()
670	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
671	            greedy_samples = torch.argmax(logprobs[long_sample_indices],
672	                                          dim=-1)
673	
674	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
675	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.
676	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[
677	                    long_sample_indices] = greedy_samples.unsqueeze(-1)
678	
679	            if modify_greedy_probs:
680	                # If required, modify the probabilities such that sampling from
681	                # the modified distribution would always sample the argmax
682	                # token id.
683	                _modify_greedy_probs_inplace(logprobs, probs,
684	                                             long_sample_indices,
685	                                             greedy_samples)
686	
687	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
688	            max_n_in_batch = 1
689	            for seq_group in seq_groups:
690	                if seq_group.is_prompt:
691	                    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
692	                    max_n_in_batch = max(max_n_in_batch, sampling_params.n)
693	            seq_groups_arg = (None if sampling_type == SamplingType.RANDOM else
694	                              seq_groups)
695	
696	            if flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is not None:
697	                logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
698	                               "per-request generators. Falling back to "
699	                               "PyTorch-native implementation.")
700	
701	            multinomial_samples[sampling_type] = _multinomial(
702	                probs[long_sample_indices],
703	                max_n_in_batch,
704	                seq_groups=seq_groups_arg)
705	
706	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
707	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.
708	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[long_sample_indices] = \
709	                    multinomial_samples[sampling_type].to(torch.long)
710	
711	        else:
712	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported sampling type: {sampling_type}")
713	
714	    # Encapsulate arguments for computing Pythonized sampler
715	    # results, whether deferred or otherwise.
716	    maybe_deferred_args = SampleResultArgsType(
717	        sampling_metadata=sampling_metadata,
718	        sample_metadata=sample_metadata,
719	        multinomial_samples=multinomial_samples,
720	        greedy_samples=greedy_samples,
721	        sample_results_dict=sample_results_dict)
722	
723	    if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
724	        # GPU<->CPU sync happens here.
725	        # This also converts the sampler output to a Python object.
726	        # Return Pythonized sampler result & sampled token ids
727	        return get_pythonized_sample_results(
728	            maybe_deferred_args), sampled_token_ids_tensor
729	    else:
730	        # Defer sampler result Pythonization; return deferred
731	        # Pythonization args & sampled token ids
732	        return (
733	            maybe_deferred_args,
734	            sampled_token_ids_tensor,
735	        )
736	
737	
738	def _sample(
739	    probs: torch.Tensor,
740	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
741	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
742	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
743	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
744	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
745	) -> SampleReturnType:
746	    """
747	    Args:
748	        probs: (num_query_tokens_in_batch, num_vocab)
749	        logprobs: (num_query_tokens_in_batch, num_vocab)
750	        sampling_metadata: The metadata for a batch for sampling.
751	        sampling_tensors: Tensors that include sampling related metadata.
752	
753	    Returns:
754	        (next_token_ids, parent_seq_ids) for each seq group in a batch.
755	            If sampling is skipped, it returns ([], [])
756	        sampled_token_ids_tensor: A tensor of sampled token ids.
757	    """
758	    return _sample_with_torch(
759	        probs,
760	        logprobs,
761	        sampling_metadata,
762	        sampling_tensors,
763	        include_gpu_probs_tensor=include_gpu_probs_tensor,
764	        modify_greedy_probs=modify_greedy_probs,
765	    )
766	
767	
768	def _get_ranks(x: torch.Tensor, indices: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
769	    """
770	    This function calculates the ranks of the chosen tokens in a logprob tensor.
771	
772	    Args:
773	        x (torch.Tensor): 2D logprob tensor of shape (N, M)
774	                        where N is the no. of tokens and M is the vocab dim.
775	        indices (torch.Tensor): List of chosen token indices.
776	
777	    Returns:
778	        torch.Tensor: 1D tensor of shape (N,) where N is the no. of tokens.
779	                    Each element in the returned tensor represents the rank
780	                    of the chosen token in the input logprob tensor.
781	    """
782	    vals = x[torch.arange(0, len(x), device=x.device, dtype=indices.dtype),
783	             indices]
784	    result = (x > vals[:, None])
785	    del vals
786	    return result.sum(1).add_(1)
787	
788	
789	def get_logprobs(
790	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
791	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
792	    sample_results: SampleResultType,
793	) -> tuple[list[Optional[PromptLogprobs]], list[SampleLogprobs]]:
794	    """Return sample logprobs and prompt logprobs.
795	
796	    The logic consists of 3 parts.
797	    - Select indices to compute logprob from, ranks of token ids, and
798	        the top k token ids from logprobs.
799	    - Compute prompt logprobs if required.
800	    - Compute sample logprobs if required.
801	
802	    Args:
803	        logprobs: (num_query_tokens_across_batch, num_vocab). Each query token's
804	            logprob per vocab. Sequence groups' query tokens are batched in a
805	            single flattened tensor. For example, assuming there are N
806	            seq groups, it is sorted by prefill tokens for seq_group_1 (if
807	            prompt logprob is enabled), decode tokens for seq_group_1 (if
808	            sampling is required), prefill tokens for seq_group_2, ...
809	        sampling_metadata: The sampling metadata.
810	        sample_results: (num_seq_groups) The tuple of (next_token_ids,
811	            parent_ids) for each sequence group. When beam search is enabled,
812	            sample_results can contain different number of seq_ids from
813	            sampling_metadata.seq_groups. It is because beam search creates
814	            2 * BEAM_WIDTH number of samples (whereas there are only up to
815	            BEAM_WIDTH number of seq_ids).
816	
817	    Returns:
818	        A tuple of prompt and sample logprobs per sequence group in a batch.
819	    """
820	    # The index of query token to calculate logprobs. It includes both
821	    # prompt and sample logprob indices.
822	    query_indices: list[int] = []
823	    # The next token ids to get the logprob value from.
824	    next_token_ids: list[int] = []
825	    # The largest requested number of logprobs. We find logprobs as many as the
826	    # largest num logprobs in this API. If every logprobs is None, it will be
827	    # set to -1.
828	    largest_num_logprobs = -1
829	
830	    # Select indices to compute logprob from, ranks of token ids, and the top
831	    # k token ids from logprobs.
832	    for (seq_group, sample_result) in zip(sampling_metadata.seq_groups,
833	                                          sample_results):
834	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
835	
836	        # Update indices and tokens for prompt logprobs.
837	        if (seq_group.is_prompt
838	                and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
839	            largest_num_logprobs = max(largest_num_logprobs,
840	                                       sampling_params.prompt_logprobs)
841	            next_prompt_tokens = _get_next_prompt_tokens(seq_group)
842	            query_indices.extend(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
843	            next_token_ids.extend(next_prompt_tokens)
844	
845	        # Update indices and next tokenes for sample logprob.
846	        if seq_group.do_sample:
847	            token_ids, parent_seq_ids = sample_result
848	            # NOTE: We cannot directly use sample_indices because
849	            # sample_indices only contain parent seq_ids of a previous step.
850	            # The current step may have different number of seq_ids, and
851	            # we can obtain it from `sample_result[1]`.
852	            query_idx = seq_group.sample_indices[0]
853	            query_indices.extend(
854	                [query_idx + parent_id for parent_id in parent_seq_ids])
855	            next_token_ids.extend(token_ids)
856	
857	            if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
858	                largest_num_logprobs = max(largest_num_logprobs,
859	                                           sampling_params.logprobs)
860	
861	        assert len(next_token_ids) == len(query_indices)
862	
863	    if len(query_indices) == 0:
864	        empty_sampled_logprob: SampleLogprobs = []
865	        empty_prompt_logprob: Optional[PromptLogprobs] = None
866	        num_seq_groups = len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups)
867	        return [empty_prompt_logprob
868	                ] * num_seq_groups, [empty_sampled_logprob] * num_seq_groups
869	
870	    selected_logprobs, ranks = None, None
871	    top_logprobs, top_token_ids = None, None
872	
873	    # If largest_num_logprobs == -1, i.e. no logprobs are requested, we can
874	    # skip the whole logprob calculation.
875	    if largest_num_logprobs >= 0:
876	        query_indices_gpu = torch.tensor(query_indices, device=logprobs.device)
877	        next_token_ids_gpu = torch.tensor(next_token_ids,
878	                                          device=logprobs.device)
879	
880	        # (num_selected_query_tokens, num_logprobs). Note that query_indices can
881	        # contain duplicates if beam search is enabled.
882	        selected_logprobs = logprobs[[
883	            query_indices_gpu,
884	            next_token_ids_gpu,
885	        ]]
886	        ranks = _get_ranks(
887	            logprobs[query_indices_gpu],
888	            next_token_ids_gpu,
889	        )
890	        assert selected_logprobs.shape[0] == ranks.shape[0]
891	
892	        # We need to compute top k only if there exists logprobs > 0.
893	        if largest_num_logprobs > 0:
894	            # Logprobs of topk tokens for a batch of sequence groups.
895	            # (num_query_tokens_across_batch).
896	            top_logprobs, top_token_ids = torch.topk(logprobs,
897	                                                     largest_num_logprobs,
898	                                                     dim=-1)
899	            top_logprobs = top_logprobs.to('cpu')

86	class SamplerOutput(
87	        msgspec.Struct,
88	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
89	        array_like=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
90	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
91	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
92	
93	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
94	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
95	    """
96	
97	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]
98	
99	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.
100	    sampled_token_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
101	
102	    # On-device tensor containing the logprobs of each token.
103	    logprobs: Optional["torch.Tensor"] = None
104	
105	    # Holds either (1) the pythonized sampler result (single-step scheduling)
106	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the
107	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)
108	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.
111	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
114	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
116	
117	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
118	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are
119	    # specified in lieu of prompt token ids or text.
120	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
121	
122	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.
123	    spec_decode_worker_metrics: Optional[SpecDecodeWorkerMetrics] = None
124	
125	    # Optional last hidden states from the model.
126	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
127	
128	    # Optional prefill hidden states from the model
129	    # (used for models like EAGLE).
130	    prefill_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
131	
132	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers
133	    model_forward_time: Optional[float] = None
134	
135	    # Time taken in the model execute function. This will include model forward,
136	    # block/sync across workers, cpu-gpu sync time and sampling time.
137	    model_execute_time: Optional[float] = None
138	
139	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
140	        return self.outputs[idx]
141	
142	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):
143	        self.outputs[idx] = value
144	
145	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]:
146	        return iter(self.outputs)
147	
148	    def __len__(self):
149	        return len(self.outputs)
150	
151	    def __eq__(self, other: object):
152	        return isinstance(other,
153	                          self.__class__) and self.outputs == other.outputs
154	
155	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
156	        """Show the shape of a tensor instead of its values to reduce noise.
157	        """
158	        sampled_token_probs_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_probs is None
159	                                    else self.sampled_token_probs.shape)
160	        sampled_token_ids_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_ids is None else
161	                                  self.sampled_token_ids.shape)
162	        return (
163	            f"SamplerOutput(outputs={self.outputs}, "
164	            f"sampled_token_probs={sampled_token_probs_repr}, "
165	            f"sampled_token_ids={sampled_token_ids_repr}, "
166	            f"spec_decode_worker_metrics={self.spec_decode_worker_metrics})")

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a

205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None

291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.

928	def _get_prompt_logprob_if_needed(
929	    seq_group: SequenceGroupToSample,
930	    selected_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
931	    ranks: torch.Tensor,
932	    top_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
933	    top_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
934	    selected_logprobs_idx: int,
935	    top_logprob_idx: int,
936	):
937	    """Compute the prompt logprob from a sequence group if needed."""
938	    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
939	    is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
940	
941	    # Find prompt logprobs
942	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[PromptLogprobs] = None
943	    if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
944	        prompt_logprobs = []
945	        num_logprobs = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
946	        next_prompt_tokens = _get_next_prompt_tokens(seq_group)
947	        # Pre-select indexes and create a list. It is faster than calling .item
948	        # repetitively.
949	        selected_logprob_items = selected_logprobs[
950	            selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
951	            len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
952	        rank_items = ranks[selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
953	                           len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
954	
955	        for idx, token_id in enumerate(next_prompt_tokens):
956	            # Calculate the prompt logprob of the real prompt tokens.
957	            # {token_id: (logprob, rank_from_vocab)}
958	            prompt_logprobs_dict: dict[int, tuple[float, int]] = {
959	                token_id: (selected_logprob_items[idx], rank_items[idx])
960	            }
961	
962	            # Add top K prompt logprobs along with its rank.
963	            if num_logprobs > 0:
964	                top_ids = top_token_ids[
965	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
966	                top_probs = top_logprobs[
967	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
968	                # Top K is already sorted by rank, so we can use 1 ~
969	                # num_logprobs + 1 for rank.
970	                top_ranks = range(1, num_logprobs + 1)
971	                prompt_logprobs_dict.update({
972	                    top_id: (top_prob, rank)
973	                    for top_id, top_prob, rank in zip(top_ids, top_probs,
974	                                                      top_ranks)
975	                })
976	            prompt_logprobs.append({
977	                token_id: Logprob(*logprob_and_rank)
978	                for token_id, logprob_and_rank in prompt_logprobs_dict.items()
979	            })
980	            # + 1 to go to the next prompt token.
981	            top_logprob_idx += 1
982	
983	        # + len(next_prompt_tokens) to go to the next prompt.
984	        selected_logprobs_idx += len(next_prompt_tokens)
985	    return prompt_logprobs, top_logprob_idx, selected_logprobs_idx

988	def _get_sampled_logprob_if_needed(
989	    seq_group: SequenceGroupToSample,
990	    sample_result: tuple[list[int], list[int]],
991	    selected_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
992	    ranks: torch.Tensor,
993	    top_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
994	    top_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
995	    selected_logprobs_idx: int,
996	    top_logprob_idx: int,
997	):
998	    """Compute the sample logprob if needed."""
999	    seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
1000	    num_logprobs = seq_group.sampling_params.logprobs
1001	    sampled_logprobs: SampleLogprobs = []
1002	    next_token_ids, parent_seq_ids = sample_result
1003	
1004	    if seq_group.do_sample:
1005	        assert len(next_token_ids) > 0
1006	        if num_logprobs is None:
1007	            for next_token_id in next_token_ids:
1008	                # Use a dummy logprob
1009	                sampled_logprobs.append({next_token_id: Logprob(inf)})
1010	        else:
1011	            # Pre-select items from tensor. tolist() is faster than repetitive
1012	            # `.item()` calls.
1013	            selected_logprob_items = selected_logprobs[
1014	                selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
1015	                len(next_token_ids)].tolist()
1016	            rank_items = ranks[selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
1017	                               len(next_token_ids)].tolist()
1018	            for idx, (next_token_id, parent_id) in enumerate(
1019	                    zip(next_token_ids, parent_seq_ids)):
1020	                # Get the logprob of a sampled token.
1021	                sampled_logprobs_dict = {
1022	                    next_token_id:
1023	                    (selected_logprob_items[idx], rank_items[idx])
1024	                }
1025	                if num_logprobs is not None and num_logprobs > 0:
1026	                    # Get top K logprobs.
1027	                    top_ids = top_token_ids[top_logprob_idx +
1028	                                            parent_id, :num_logprobs].tolist()
1029	                    top_probs = top_logprobs[
1030	                        top_logprob_idx + parent_id, :num_logprobs].tolist()
1031	                    # Top K is already sorted by rank, so we can use 1 ~
1032	                    # num_logprobs + 1 for rank.
1033	                    top_ranks = range(1, num_logprobs + 1)
1034	                    sampled_logprobs_dict.update({
1035	                        top_id: (top_prob, rank)
1036	                        for top_id, top_prob, rank in zip(
1037	                            top_ids, top_probs, top_ranks)
1038	                    })
1039	
1040	                sampled_logprobs.append({
1041	                    token_id: Logprob(*logprob_and_rank)
1042	                    for token_id, logprob_and_rank in
1043	                    sampled_logprobs_dict.items()
1044	                })
1045	
1046	        # NOTE: This part of code is not intuitive. `selected_logprobs` include
1047	        # logprobs for the current step, which has len(next_token_ids) tokens
1048	        # per sequence group. `logprobs` includes logprobs from the previous
1049	        # steps, which has len(seq_ids) tokens per sequence group.
1050	
1051	        # Iterate to the next sequence group in a batch.
1052	        selected_logprobs_idx += len(next_token_ids)
1053	        # Iterate to the next sequence group in a batch.
1054	        top_logprob_idx += len(seq_ids)
1055	    return sampled_logprobs, top_logprob_idx, selected_logprobs_idx

1058	def _modify_greedy_probs_inplace(logprobs: torch.Tensor, probs: torch.Tensor,
1059	                                 sample_indices: torch.Tensor,
1060	                                 greedy_samples: torch.Tensor) -> None:
1061	    """Modify the probability distributions of the greedily-sampled tokens such
1062	    that each sampled token has a "probability" of 1.0. This is required by
1063	    speculative decoding, which depends on the sampling method being encoded
1064	    within the probability distribution for correctness.
1065	
1066	    # Why do we only need to do this for greedy sampling?
1067	
1068	    vLLM's sampler performs the following steps for greedy or multinomial
1069	    (random) sampling:
1070	        1. Get logits from model.
1071	        2. Modify logits according to per-sequence sampling parameters.
1072	            - Multiply by temperature, top-k and top-p masking, penalize tokens
1073	                according to their frequency, etc.
1074	        3. Sample a token.
1075	            - Random sampling simply samples from the modified probability
1076	                distribution.
1077	            - Greedy sampling performs `argmax` to obtain the token with the
1078	                highest likelihood.
1079	
1080	    Ignoring greedy sampling for a moment, we find that the computed probability
1081	    distribution has the following property: we can sample from it independently
1082	    and find that the token sampled by the Sampler has a frequency corresponding
1083	    to how often we see it in our sampling. In other words, for tokens sampled
1084	    with vLLM's random SamplingType, the computed probability distribution
1085	    encodes the sampling methodology completely.
1086	
1087	    Greedy sampling does not normally have this property. vLLM modifies logits
1088	    according to sampling params, then performs `argmax`, then returns the
1089	    sampled token and the computed probability distribution. If we sample from
1090	    the distribution, we'll find the likelihood of the greedily-sampled token
1091	    is not always 1.0.
1092	
1093	    Since lossless speculative decoding requires that the sampling methodology
1094	    be encoded within the probability distribution, we are motivated to modify
1095	    the probability distribution such that the sampled token has probability 1
1096	    when speculative decoding is used.
1097	
1098	    NOTE: Alternatively, we could use an extremely low temperature to achieve
1099	    greedy sampling using multinomial computation and unite the codepaths. This
1100	    has implications on the overall design of the sampler, e.g. how to record
1101	    accurate logprobs for the user, so this improvement is deferred to later.
1102	    """
1103	    # NOTE: logprobs are not modified so they can be returned to the user.
1104	    probs[sample_indices, :] = 0
1105	    probs[sample_indices, greedy_samples] = 1.0
1106

1122	            allows post-processing without copies to CPU/serialization, e.g. in

1145	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids

1149	                    parent_ids, next_token_ids, group_sample_logprobs):
1150	                seq_outputs.append(

1152	                                   logprobs))
1153	            sampler_output.append(
1154	                CompletionSequenceGroupOutput(seq_outputs,
1155	                                              group_prompt_logprobs))
1156	
1157	    # If not specified, store None values in SamplerOutput.
1158	    if on_device_tensors is not None:

1160	         sampled_token_ids) = on_device_tensors
1161	    else:
1162	        sampled_token_probs, logprobs_tensor, sampled_token_ids = (None, None,
1163	                                                                   None)
1164	
1165	    return SamplerOutput(
1166	        outputs=sampler_output,
1167	        sampled_token_probs=sampled_token_probs,
1168	        sampled_token_ids=sampled_token_ids,
1169	        logprobs=logprobs_tensor,
1170	        deferred_sample_results_args=deferred_sample_results_args)

614	def _sample_with_torch(
615	    probs: torch.Tensor,
616	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
617	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
618	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
619	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
620	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
621	) -> SampleReturnType:
622	    '''Torch-oriented _sample() implementation.
623	
624	    Single-step scheduling:
625	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
626	    * Immediately Pythonize sampling result
627	
628	    Multi-step scheduling:
629	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
630	    * Defer Pythonization & preserve GPU-side
631	      tensors required for Pythonization

634	    categorized_seq_group_ids: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
635	        t: []
636	        for t in SamplingType
637	    }
638	    categorized_sample_indices = sampling_metadata.categorized_sample_indices
639	    for i, seq_group in enumerate(sampling_metadata.seq_groups):
640	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
641	        sampling_type = sampling_params.sampling_type
642	        categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type].append(i)
643	
644	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType = {}
645	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType = {}
646	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType = {}
647	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
648	
649	    # Create output tensor for sampled token ids.
650	    if include_gpu_probs_tensor:
651	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = torch.full((logprobs.shape[0], 1),
652	                                              VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
653	                                              dtype=torch.long,
654	                                              device=logprobs.device)
655	    else:
656	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = None

658	    # Counterintiutively, having two loops here is actually faster.
659	    # The first loop can run without waiting on GPU<->CPU sync.
660	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
661	        sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices[sampling_type]
662	        num_tokens = len(sample_indices)
663	        if num_tokens == 0:

665	
666	        seq_group_id = categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type]
667	        seq_groups = [sampling_metadata.seq_groups[i] for i in seq_group_id]

672	                                          dim=-1)

676	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[

679	            if modify_greedy_probs:

683	                _modify_greedy_probs_inplace(logprobs, probs,

685	                                             greedy_samples)
686	
687	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
688	            max_n_in_batch = 1
689	            for seq_group in seq_groups:
690	                if seq_group.is_prompt:
691	                    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
692	                    max_n_in_batch = max(max_n_in_batch, sampling_params.n)
693	            seq_groups_arg = (None if sampling_type == SamplingType.RANDOM else
694	                              seq_groups)
695	
696	            if flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is not None:
697	                logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
698	                               "per-request generators. Falling back to "
699	                               "PyTorch-native implementation.")
700	
701	            multinomial_samples[sampling_type] = _multinomial(
702	                probs[long_sample_indices],
703	                max_n_in_batch,
704	                seq_groups=seq_groups_arg)
705	
706	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
707	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.
708	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[long_sample_indices] = \
709	                    multinomial_samples[sampling_type].to(torch.long)
710	
711	        else:
712	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported sampling type: {sampling_type}")
713	
714	    # Encapsulate arguments for computing Pythonized sampler
715	    # results, whether deferred or otherwise.
716	    maybe_deferred_args = SampleResultArgsType(
717	        sampling_metadata=sampling_metadata,
718	        sample_metadata=sample_metadata,
719	        multinomial_samples=multinomial_samples,
720	        greedy_samples=greedy_samples,
721	        sample_results_dict=sample_results_dict)
722	
723	    if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
724	        # GPU<->CPU sync happens here.
725	        # This also converts the sampler output to a Python object.
726	        # Return Pythonized sampler result & sampled token ids
727	        return get_pythonized_sample_results(
728	            maybe_deferred_args), sampled_token_ids_tensor
729	    else:
730	        # Defer sampler result Pythonization; return deferred
731	        # Pythonization args & sampled token ids
732	        return (
733	            maybe_deferred_args,
734	            sampled_token_ids_tensor,
735	        )
736	
737	
738	def _sample(
739	    probs: torch.Tensor,
740	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
741	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
742	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
743	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
744	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
745	) -> SampleReturnType:
746	    """
747	    Args:
748	        probs: (num_query_tokens_in_batch, num_vocab)
749	        logprobs: (num_query_tokens_in_batch, num_vocab)
750	        sampling_metadata: The metadata for a batch for sampling.
751	        sampling_tensors: Tensors that include sampling related metadata.
752	
753	    Returns:
754	        (next_token_ids, parent_seq_ids) for each seq group in a batch.
755	            If sampling is skipped, it returns ([], [])
756	        sampled_token_ids_tensor: A tensor of sampled token ids.
757	    """
758	    return _sample_with_torch(
759	        probs,
760	        logprobs,
761	        sampling_metadata,
762	        sampling_tensors,
763	        include_gpu_probs_tensor=include_gpu_probs_tensor,
764	        modify_greedy_probs=modify_greedy_probs,
765	    )
766	
767	
768	def _get_ranks(x: torch.Tensor, indices: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
769	    """
770	    This function calculates the ranks of the chosen tokens in a logprob tensor.
771	
772	    Args:
773	        x (torch.Tensor): 2D logprob tensor of shape (N, M)
774	                        where N is the no. of tokens and M is the vocab dim.
775	        indices (torch.Tensor): List of chosen token indices.
776	
777	    Returns:
778	        torch.Tensor: 1D tensor of shape (N,) where N is the no. of tokens.
779	                    Each element in the returned tensor represents the rank
780	                    of the chosen token in the input logprob tensor.
781	    """
782	    vals = x[torch.arange(0, len(x), device=x.device, dtype=indices.dtype),
783	             indices]
784	    result = (x > vals[:, None])
785	    del vals
786	    return result.sum(1).add_(1)
787	
788	
789	def get_logprobs(
790	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
791	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
792	    sample_results: SampleResultType,
793	) -> tuple[list[Optional[PromptLogprobs]], list[SampleLogprobs]]:
794	    """Return sample logprobs and prompt logprobs.
795	
796	    The logic consists of 3 parts.
797	    - Select indices to compute logprob from, ranks of token ids, and
798	        the top k token ids from logprobs.
799	    - Compute prompt logprobs if required.
800	    - Compute sample logprobs if required.
801	
802	    Args:
803	        logprobs: (num_query_tokens_across_batch, num_vocab). Each query token's
804	            logprob per vocab. Sequence groups' query tokens are batched in a
805	            single flattened tensor. For example, assuming there are N
806	            seq groups, it is sorted by prefill tokens for seq_group_1 (if
807	            prompt logprob is enabled), decode tokens for seq_group_1 (if
808	            sampling is required), prefill tokens for seq_group_2, ...
809	        sampling_metadata: The sampling metadata.
810	        sample_results: (num_seq_groups) The tuple of (next_token_ids,
811	            parent_ids) for each sequence group. When beam search is enabled,
812	            sample_results can contain different number of seq_ids from
813	            sampling_metadata.seq_groups. It is because beam search creates
814	            2 * BEAM_WIDTH number of samples (whereas there are only up to
815	            BEAM_WIDTH number of seq_ids).
816	
817	    Returns:
818	        A tuple of prompt and sample logprobs per sequence group in a batch.
819	    """
820	    # The index of query token to calculate logprobs. It includes both
821	    # prompt and sample logprob indices.
822	    query_indices: list[int] = []
823	    # The next token ids to get the logprob value from.
824	    next_token_ids: list[int] = []
825	    # The largest requested number of logprobs. We find logprobs as many as the
826	    # largest num logprobs in this API. If every logprobs is None, it will be
827	    # set to -1.
828	    largest_num_logprobs = -1
829	
830	    # Select indices to compute logprob from, ranks of token ids, and the top
831	    # k token ids from logprobs.
832	    for (seq_group, sample_result) in zip(sampling_metadata.seq_groups,
833	                                          sample_results):
834	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
835	
836	        # Update indices and tokens for prompt logprobs.
837	        if (seq_group.is_prompt
838	                and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
839	            largest_num_logprobs = max(largest_num_logprobs,
840	                                       sampling_params.prompt_logprobs)
841	            next_prompt_tokens = _get_next_prompt_tokens(seq_group)
842	            query_indices.extend(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
843	            next_token_ids.extend(next_prompt_tokens)
844	
845	        # Update indices and next tokenes for sample logprob.
846	        if seq_group.do_sample:
847	            token_ids, parent_seq_ids = sample_result
848	            # NOTE: We cannot directly use sample_indices because
849	            # sample_indices only contain parent seq_ids of a previous step.
850	            # The current step may have different number of seq_ids, and
851	            # we can obtain it from `sample_result[1]`.
852	            query_idx = seq_group.sample_indices[0]
853	            query_indices.extend(
854	                [query_idx + parent_id for parent_id in parent_seq_ids])
855	            next_token_ids.extend(token_ids)
856	
857	            if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
858	                largest_num_logprobs = max(largest_num_logprobs,
859	                                           sampling_params.logprobs)
860	
861	        assert len(next_token_ids) == len(query_indices)
862	
863	    if len(query_indices) == 0:
864	        empty_sampled_logprob: SampleLogprobs = []
865	        empty_prompt_logprob: Optional[PromptLogprobs] = None
866	        num_seq_groups = len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups)
867	        return [empty_prompt_logprob
868	                ] * num_seq_groups, [empty_sampled_logprob] * num_seq_groups
869	
870	    selected_logprobs, ranks = None, None
871	    top_logprobs, top_token_ids = None, None
872	
873	    # If largest_num_logprobs == -1, i.e. no logprobs are requested, we can
874	    # skip the whole logprob calculation.
875	    if largest_num_logprobs >= 0:
876	        query_indices_gpu = torch.tensor(query_indices, device=logprobs.device)
877	        next_token_ids_gpu = torch.tensor(next_token_ids,
878	                                          device=logprobs.device)
879	
880	        # (num_selected_query_tokens, num_logprobs). Note that query_indices can
881	        # contain duplicates if beam search is enabled.
882	        selected_logprobs = logprobs[[
883	            query_indices_gpu,
884	            next_token_ids_gpu,
885	        ]]
886	        ranks = _get_ranks(
887	            logprobs[query_indices_gpu],
888	            next_token_ids_gpu,
889	        )
890	        assert selected_logprobs.shape[0] == ranks.shape[0]
891	
892	        # We need to compute top k only if there exists logprobs > 0.
893	        if largest_num_logprobs > 0:
894	            # Logprobs of topk tokens for a batch of sequence groups.
895	            # (num_query_tokens_across_batch).
896	            top_logprobs, top_token_ids = torch.topk(logprobs,
897	                                                     largest_num_logprobs,
898	                                                     dim=-1)
899	            top_logprobs = top_logprobs.to('cpu')
900	            top_token_ids = top_token_ids.to('cpu')
901	
902	        selected_logprobs = selected_logprobs.to('cpu')
903	        ranks = ranks.to('cpu')
904	
905	    # Find prompt/sample logprobs.
906	    prompt_logprobs_per_seq_group: list[Optional[PromptLogprobs]] = []
907	    sample_logprobs_per_seq_group: list[SampleLogprobs] = []
908	    top_logprob_idx = 0
909	    selected_logprobs_idx = 0
910	
911	    for seq_group, sample_result in zip(sampling_metadata.seq_groups,
912	                                        sample_results):
913	        (prompt_logprobs, top_logprob_idx,
914	         selected_logprobs_idx) = _get_prompt_logprob_if_needed(
915	             seq_group, selected_logprobs, ranks, top_token_ids, top_logprobs,
916	             selected_logprobs_idx, top_logprob_idx)
917	        prompt_logprobs_per_seq_group.append(prompt_logprobs)
918	
919	        (sampled_logprobs, top_logprob_idx,
920	         selected_logprobs_idx) = _get_sampled_logprob_if_needed(
921	             seq_group, sample_result, selected_logprobs, ranks, top_token_ids,
922	             top_logprobs, selected_logprobs_idx, top_logprob_idx)
923	        sample_logprobs_per_seq_group.append(sampled_logprobs)
924	
925	    return prompt_logprobs_per_seq_group, sample_logprobs_per_seq_group
926	
927	
928	def _get_prompt_logprob_if_needed(
929	    seq_group: SequenceGroupToSample,
930	    selected_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
931	    ranks: torch.Tensor,
932	    top_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
933	    top_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
934	    selected_logprobs_idx: int,
935	    top_logprob_idx: int,
936	):
937	    """Compute the prompt logprob from a sequence group if needed."""
938	    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
939	    is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
940	
941	    # Find prompt logprobs
942	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[PromptLogprobs] = None
943	    if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
944	        prompt_logprobs = []
945	        num_logprobs = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
946	        next_prompt_tokens = _get_next_prompt_tokens(seq_group)
947	        # Pre-select indexes and create a list. It is faster than calling .item
948	        # repetitively.
949	        selected_logprob_items = selected_logprobs[
950	            selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
951	            len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
952	        rank_items = ranks[selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
953	                           len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
954	
955	        for idx, token_id in enumerate(next_prompt_tokens):
956	            # Calculate the prompt logprob of the real prompt tokens.
957	            # {token_id: (logprob, rank_from_vocab)}
958	            prompt_logprobs_dict: dict[int, tuple[float, int]] = {
959	                token_id: (selected_logprob_items[idx], rank_items[idx])
960	            }
961	
962	            # Add top K prompt logprobs along with its rank.
963	            if num_logprobs > 0:
964	                top_ids = top_token_ids[
965	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
966	                top_probs = top_logprobs[
967	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
968	                # Top K is already sorted by rank, so we can use 1 ~
969	                # num_logprobs + 1 for rank.
970	                top_ranks = range(1, num_logprobs + 1)
971	                prompt_logprobs_dict.update({
972	                    top_id: (top_prob, rank)
973	                    for top_id, top_prob, rank in zip(top_ids, top_probs,
974	                                                      top_ranks)
975	                })
976	            prompt_logprobs.append({
977	                token_id: Logprob(*logprob_and_rank)
978	                for token_id, logprob_and_rank in prompt_logprobs_dict.items()
979	            })
980	            # + 1 to go to the next prompt token.
981	            top_logprob_idx += 1
982	
983	        # + len(next_prompt_tokens) to go to the next prompt.
984	        selected_logprobs_idx += len(next_prompt_tokens)
985	    return prompt_logprobs, top_logprob_idx, selected_logprobs_idx
986	
987	
988	def _get_sampled_logprob_if_needed(
989	    seq_group: SequenceGroupToSample,
990	    sample_result: tuple[list[int], list[int]],
991	    selected_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
992	    ranks: torch.Tensor,
993	    top_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
994	    top_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
995	    selected_logprobs_idx: int,
996	    top_logprob_idx: int,
997	):
998	    """Compute the sample logprob if needed."""
999	    seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids

3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
14	
15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
16	
17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187	
188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
201	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
202	            )
203	        return logits
204	
205	    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
206	        self,
207	        logits: torch.Tensor,
208	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
209	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
210	        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
211	            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
212	                                float("-inf"))
213	        return logits
214	
215	    def apply_bad_words(
216	        self,
217	        logits: torch.Tensor,
218	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
219	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
220	        if sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids:
221	            apply_bad_words(
222	                logits,
223	                sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids,
224	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
225	            )
226	        return logits
227

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results
477	
478	
479	def _random_sample(
480	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
481	    random_samples: torch.Tensor,
482	) -> SampleResultType:
483	    """Run random sampling on a given samples.
484	
485	    Args:
486	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
487	        random_samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The
488	            length of samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
489	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
490	    Returns:
491	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
492	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
493	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
494	    """
495	    # Find the maximum n value of the prompt phase requests.
496	    random_samples = random_samples.cpu()
497	    sample_idx = 0
498	    results: SampleResultType = []
499	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
500	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
501	            results.append(([], []))
502	            continue
503	
504	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
505	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
506	        is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
507	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
508	        if is_prompt:
509	            # Prompt phase.
510	            parent_ids = [0] * sampling_params.n
511	            next_token_ids = random_samples[
512	                sample_idx, :sampling_params.n].tolist()
513	        else:
514	            # Generation phase.
515	            parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
516	            next_token_ids = random_samples[sample_idx:sample_idx +
517	                                            num_parent_seqs, 0].tolist()
518	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
519	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
520	    return results
521	
522	
523	# torch.multinomial forces a GPU<->CPU sync.
524	# Therefore, we use an optimized implementation instead.
525	# Note that we always sample with replacement.
526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)
