22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)

200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209

211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

57	def test_flashinfer_sampler():
58	    '''
59	    This test verifies that the FlashInfer top-k and top-p sampling
60	    implementation produces the same results as the Python implementation.
61	
62	    NOTE: FlashInfer did not directly expose an interface for fused top-k and
63	    top-p prob renorm (it did provide fused sampling but we cannot compare
64	    sampling results due to randomness), so we will compare the probability
65	    renormed consequently by top-k and then top-p of FlashInfer implementation.
66	    '''
67	
68	    if not FLASHINFER_ENABLED:
69	        pytest.skip(
70	            "FlashInfer not installed or not available on this platform.")
71	
72	    torch.set_default_device(DEVICE)
73	    generator = Generator(device=DEVICE).manual_seed(42)
74	
75	    # Generate random logits
76	    logits = torch.rand((BATCH_SIZE, VOCAB_SIZE), generator=generator)
77	
78	    # Generate various top-k and top-p values
79	    k_values = torch.randint(1, 1000, (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator)
80	    p_values = torch.rand(
81	        (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator) * 0.5 + 0.5  # range in [0.5, 1.0]
82	
83	    # Sometimes disable top-k (k=vocab_size)
84	    k_values.masked_fill_(
85	        torch.randint(0,
86	                      2, (BATCH_SIZE, ),
87	                      generator=generator,
88	                      dtype=torch.bool), VOCAB_SIZE)
89	
90	    # Sometimes disable top-p (p=1.0)
91	    p_values.masked_fill_(
92	        torch.randint(0,
93	                      2, (BATCH_SIZE, ),
94	                      generator=generator,
95	                      dtype=torch.bool), 1.0)
96	
97	    python_logits = apply_top_k_top_p(
98	        logits=logits.clone(),
99	        k=k_values,
100	        p=p_values,
101	    )
102	    python_probs = torch.softmax(python_logits, dim=-1)
103	
104	    # FlashInfer only exposed renorm interfaces for probs so convert first
105	    flashinfer_probs = torch.softmax(logits.clone(), dim=-1)
106	    flashinfer_probs = top_k_renorm_probs(
107	        probs=flashinfer_probs,
108	        top_k=k_values,
109	    )
110	    flashinfer_probs = top_p_renorm_probs(
111	        probs=flashinfer_probs,
112	        top_p=p_values,
113	    )
114	
115	    # Compare the results
116	    assert torch.allclose(python_probs, flashinfer_probs, atol=2e-2), \
117	        "FlashInfer and Python sampling implementations do not match!"
118

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document for you. Let me gather all the information and provide it in a structured format.

## Summary: vLLM Sampler/Logits Processor Pipeline Analysis

I've explored the vLLM sampling pipeline in detail. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of the architecture:

---

### 1. FILES CONTAINING "top_p" AND "top_k"

201 files across the vLLM codebase contain these strings, including:
- Core sampler files: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`
- Metadata files: `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, `vllm/sampling_params.py`
- Worker/executor files for different hardware (GPU, TPU, HPU, Neuron, XPU)
- Sampling-related test files

---

### 2. MAIN SAMPLER FILE (V0 - Production)
**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

This is the main sampler for single-step scheduling. Key structure:

**Key Classes:**
- `Sampler(nn.Module)` - Main sampling layer
- `SamplerOutput` - msgspec struct with outputs and optional tensors
- `SampleResultArgsType` - Dataclass for deferred sample results

**Main Flow in `forward()`:**
1. Initialize sampling tensors with penalties, temperatures, top_k, top_p, min_p
2. Apply min_tokens penalty
3. Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
4. Apply temperature scaling (in-place division)
5. Apply top-k/top-p truncation (either built-in or via FlashInfer)
6. Apply min_p filtering
7. Compute softmax probabilities and log-softmax
8. Call `_sample()` to perform actual sampling

**Key helper functions:**
- `_apply_min_tokens_penalty()` - Set stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens not reached
- `_apply_top_k_top_p()` - Truncate logits based on top-k and top-p
- `_apply_min_p()` - Filter tokens below minimum probability threshold
- `_multinomial()` - Optimized multinomial sampling (with optional per-sequence generators)
- `_sample_with_torch()` - Core sampling logic (greedy, random, random_seed)
- `_greedy_sample()` - Argmax sampling
- `_random_sample()` - Multinomial sampling from probabilities

---

### 3. LOGITS PROCESSOR FILE
**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py`

**Key Class:**
- `LogitsProcessor(nn.Module)` - Processes logits before sampling

**Main Flow in `forward()`:**
1. Prune hidden states if needed (select only tokens to compute logits for)
2. Get logits from LM head via `_get_logits()`
3. Apply soft cap (for models like Gemma 2)
4. Apply logits scaling
5. Apply custom logits processors if provided

**Key helper functions:**
- `_prune_hidden_states()` - Select only needed token indices
- `_apply_logits_processors()` - Apply per-sequence custom logits processors
- `_apply_logits_processors_single_seq()` - Apply processors to single sequence
- Thread-based parallel processing support via `ThreadPoolExecutor`

---

### 4. SAMPLING PARAMETERS
**File:** `/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Key Class:**
- `SamplingParams` (msgspec.Struct) - User-facing sampling configuration

**Fields:**
```python
n: int = 1
best_of: Optional[int] = None
presence_penalty: float = 0.0
frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
temperature: float = 1.0
top_p: float = 1.0
top_k: int = 0
min_p: float = 0.0
seed: Optional[int] = None
logprobs: Optional[int] = None
prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
# ... plus many control fields
```

**Key Methods:**
- `from_optional()` - Factory method to create params with defaults
- `sampling_type` property - Returns SamplingType (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)

---

### 5. SAMPLING METADATA (INTERMEDIATE DATA STRUCTURE)
**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Key Classes:**

#### SequenceGroupToSample
```python
@dataclass
class SequenceGroupToSample:
    seq_ids: list[int]
    sampling_params: SamplingParams
    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
    seq_len: Optional[int]
    query_len: Optional[int]
    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
    is_prompt: bool
    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
    sample_indices: list[int]
```

#### SamplingMetadata
Main intermediary passed from scheduler to sampler:
```python
class SamplingMetadata:
    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]  # Batched sequence groups
    selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor     # Which tokens to get logits for
    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]  # Per-type indices
    num_prompts: int
    skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool
    reuse_sampling_tensors: bool
```

#### SamplingTensors
Packed tensors for efficient GPU processing:
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Key Method:**
- `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` - Converts metadata to tensors
- `SamplingMetadata.prepare()` - Creates metadata from sequence group metadata list

---

### 6. V1 SAMPLER (NEW/EXPERIMENTAL)
**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`

Simplified architecture with key differences:
- Uses `TopKTopPSampler` class for top-k/top-p operations
- Separates logits processors by type (argmax_invariant vs non_argmax_invariant)
- Cleaner separation of concerns: penalty application, sampling, logprobs gathering

**Main Flow:**
1. Compute raw logprobs (before any modifications)
2. Apply allowed token IDs mask
3. Apply bad words exclusion
4. Apply non-argmax-invariant logits processors
5. Apply penalties
6. Sample (with temperature, argmax-invariant processors, top-k/top-p)
7. Gather logprobs for top-k and sampled tokens

---

### 7. V1 METADATA
**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

Simpler dataclass-based design:
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]
    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager
```

---

### 8. KEY DATA FLOW

**V0 Pipeline (Production):**
```
Scheduler creates SequenceGroupMetadata list
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.prepare() creates:
  - SequenceGroupToSample objects
  - selected_token_indices tensor
  - categorized_sample_indices (per SamplingType)
    ↓
LogitsProcessor.forward():
  - Prunes hidden states
  - Gets logits from LM head
  - Applies custom logits processors
    ↓
Sampler.forward():
  - SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata() packs tensors
  - Applies min_tokens penalty
  - Applies penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
  - Temperature scaling
  - Top-k/top-p truncation
  - Compute softmax/log_softmax
  - _sample_with_torch():
    * Categorizes by sampling type
    * Greedy: argmax on logprobs
    * Random: multinomial sampling from probs
  - Compute logprobs for top-k and sampled tokens
    ↓
SamplerOutput with CompletionSequenceGroupOutput
```

**Critical Data Flow for top_p/top_k:**
1. `SamplingParams.top_p` and `SamplingParams.top_k` per request
2. Packed into `SamplingTensors.top_ps` and `SamplingTensors.top_ks`
3. Applied in `_apply_top_k_top_p()` (vLLM native) or via FlashInfer
4. Masks set to -inf for tokens outside top-k/top-p
5. Probabilities recomputed from masked logits

---

### 9. SPECIAL CASES & FEATURES

**Temperature Handling:**
- Zero temperature (< 1e-5): Greedy sampling, top_p/top_k overridden to defaults
- Non-zero: Logits divided by temperature before softmax

**Multi-step Scheduling Support:**
- `SampleResultArgsType` defers pythonization of results
- GPU tensors preserved for later batch processing
- `reuse_sampling_tensors` flag allows tensor reuse across decode steps

**Seeded Randomness:**
- Per-request generators stored in `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`
- Used in `_multinomial()` for reproducible random sampling

**Logprobs Computation:**
- Stored as GPU tensors initially
- Transferred to CPU in `get_logprobs()`
- Top-k computed separately from sampled token logprobs
- Includes token rank information

---

This architecture provides efficient batched sampling with per-sequence customization while maintaining GPU performance through careful tensor packing and asynchronous CPU transfers.

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Summary: How top_p and top_k Sampling Parameters are Implemented in vLLM

Based on my exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how top_p and top_k sampling parameters are implemented:

---

### 1. SAMPLING PARAMETERS CLASS DEFINITION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331675740-9yvs7b/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Field Definitions (lines 212-213):**
```python
top_p: float = 1.0
top_k: int = 0
```

**Key characteristics:**
- `top_p`: Float type, default value 1.0 (no filtering)
- `top_k`: Integer type, default value 0 (no filtering; -1 also accepted)

**Documentation (lines 150-153 in docstring):**
```
top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
    to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
    to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
```

**Validation Logic (lines 417-425):**
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")
if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```

**Special Behavior (lines 377-382):**
When temperature is very low (greedy sampling), top_p and top_k are automatically disabled:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

**Conversion from Request (from_optional method, lines 259-304):**
- Default is 1.0 for top_p and 0 for top_k
- logit_bias values are clamped to [-100, 100] range per OpenAI API spec

---

### 2. SAMPLING METADATA & TENSOR PREPARATION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331675740-9yvs7b/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**SamplingTensors Data Structure (lines 372-385):**
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor        # Shape: (num_samples,)
    top_ks: torch.Tensor        # Shape: (num_samples,)
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Key Preparation Logic (lines 387-495):**
- `from_sampling_metadata()` method extracts top_p and top_k from sampling parameters into per-sequence tensors
- Lines 415-420: top_k is clamped to vocabulary size:
  ```python
  top_p = sampling_params.top_p
  min_p = sampling_params.min_p
  # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
  top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
  top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
  ```
- Lines 426-428: Sets `do_top_p_top_k` flag if either parameter is active:
  ```python
  if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                             or top_k != vocab_size):
      do_top_p_top_k = True
  ```

### 4. TOP_P AND TOP_K FILTERING IMPLEMENTATION (V0)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331675740-9yvs7b/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`


**_apply_top_k_top_p() Function (lines 398-424):**

The core filtering logic that operates on logits:

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,        # Shape: (batch_size, vocab_size)
    p: torch.Tensor,             # Shape: (batch_size,)
    k: torch.Tensor,             # Shape: (batch_size,)
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Apply top-k masking
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Apply top-p masking
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Math explained:**
- **Top-K**: Sort logits in ascending order, mask out all values below the k-th largest
- **Top-P**: Sort logits, compute cumulative probability, mask out tokens until cumulative probability exceeds (1-p)
- **At least one**: Even if cumulative probability exceeds threshold, always keep at least one token

**_apply_min_p() Function (lines 427-441):**
Applies minimum probability threshold (relative to max probability):
```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**apply_top_k_top_p() Function (lines 168-208):**
PyTorch-native implementation with optimizations:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
    
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**apply_top_k_only() Optimization (lines 211-233):**
For top_k-only filtering, avoids sorting the entire vocabulary:
```python
def apply_top_k_only(logits: torch.Tensor, k: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**apply_top_k_top_p_tpu() Function (lines 121-165):**
TPU-optimized version that avoids slow scatter operations:
- Uses cumulative probability to find a "cut-off" element
- Thresholds logits based on this cutoff rather than using scatter

**FlashInfer Integration (lines 260-296):**
Optional high-performance implementation using the FlashInfer library:
```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if k is None:
        # Top-p only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        # Top-k only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        # Both top-k and top-p
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

Example test structure:
```python
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_default_device():
    original_device = torch.get_default_device()
    yield
    torch.set_default_device(original_device)

def test_topk_impl_equivalence():
    torch.set_default_device(DEVICE)
    generator = Generator(device=DEVICE).manual_seed(33)
    
    logits = torch.rand((BATCH_SIZE, VOCAB_SIZE), generator=generator)
    k = torch.randint(1, 10, (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator)
    k.masked_fill_(torch.randint(0, 2, (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator, dtype=bool),
                   VOCAB_SIZE)
    
    result1 = apply_top_k_top_p(logits=logits.clone(), k=k, p=None)
    result2 = apply_top_k_top_p(logits=logits.clone(), k=k, p=torch.tensor([1.0]))
    assert torch.allclose(result1, result2)
```

| `tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py` | Functional tests | Equivalence, FlashInfer comparison, large-scale tests |

```
    ↓
Validation (_verify_args) 
    ↓

    - Extract top_p, top_k per sequence

Sampler.forward()
    - Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)

Sampling (_sample, _multinomial)

SamplerOutput
```

## Summary: How top_p and top_k Sampling Parameters are Implemented in vLLM

Based on my exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how top_p and top_k sampling parameters are implemented:

---

### 1. SAMPLING PARAMETERS CLASS DEFINITION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331675740-9yvs7b/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Field Definitions (lines 212-213):**
```python
top_p: float = 1.0
top_k: int = 0
```

**Key characteristics:**
- `top_p`: Float type, default value 1.0 (no filtering)
- `top_k`: Integer type, default value 0 (no filtering; -1 also accepted)

**Documentation (lines 150-153 in docstring):**
```
top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
    to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
    to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
```

**Validation Logic (lines 417-425):**
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")
if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```

**Special Behavior (lines 377-382):**
When temperature is very low (greedy sampling), top_p and top_k are automatically disabled:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

**Conversion from Request (from_optional method, lines 259-304):**
- Default is 1.0 for top_p and 0 for top_k
- logit_bias values are clamped to [-100, 100] range per OpenAI API spec

---

### 2. SAMPLING METADATA & TENSOR PREPARATION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331675740-9yvs7b/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

```python
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
```

```python
def test_topk_impl_equivalence():
    torch.set_default_device(DEVICE)
    generator = Generator(device=DEVICE).manual_seed(33)
    
    logits = torch.rand((BATCH_SIZE, VOCAB_SIZE), generator=generator)
    k = torch.randint(1, 10, (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator)
    k.masked_fill_(torch.randint(0, 2, (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator, dtype=bool),
                   VOCAB_SIZE)
    
    result1 = apply_top_k_top_p(logits=logits.clone(), k=k, p=None)
    result2 = apply_top_k_top_p(logits=logits.clone(), k=k, p=torch.tensor([1.0]))
    assert torch.allclose(result1, result2)
```

### KEY FILES SUMMARY TABLE

| File | Purpose | Key Content |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Parameter definition & validation | Field definitions, bounds checking, docstrings |
| `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | Tensor preparation | `SamplingTensors` dataclass, per-sample tensor creation |
| `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | V0 sampling execution | `_apply_top_k_top_p()`, `_apply_min_p()` functions |
| `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | V1 sampling execution | Multiple implementations (native, TPU, FlashInfer) |
| `tests/test_sampling_params.py` | Parameter validation tests | Basic sanity checks |
| `tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py` | Functional tests | Equivalence, FlashInfer comparison, large-scale tests |

### EXECUTION FLOW SUMMARY

```
SamplingParams (user input)
    ↓
Validation (_verify_args) 
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    - Extract top_p, top_k per sequence
    - Create pinned memory tensors
    ↓
Sampler.forward()
    - Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
    - Apply temperature scaling
    - **Apply top_p/top_k filtering** (_apply_top_k_top_p)
    - Apply min_p filtering
    - Compute probabilities (softmax)
    ↓
Sampling (_sample, _multinomial)
    - Greedy sampling: argmax
    - Random sampling: multinomial
    ↓
SamplerOutput
```

This comprehensive overview shows how vLLM implements top_p and top_k as parallel filtering operations on logits before converting to probabilities and sampling.

frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
496	
497	    @classmethod
498	    def from_lists(
499	        cls,
500	        temperatures: list[float],
501	        top_ps: list[float],
502	        top_ks: list[int],
503	        min_ps: list[float],
504	        presence_penalties: list[float],
505	        frequency_penalties: list[float],

514	        # pinned memory.

516	
517	        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens
518	
519	        if do_penalties:

521	                prompt_tokens,

528	                output_tokens,
529	                vocab_size,
530	                device="cpu",
531	                dtype=torch.int64,
532	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
533	            )
534	        else:
535	            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
536	            prompt_t = empty_tensor
537	            output_t = empty_tensor
538	
539	        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
540	            temperatures,
541	            device="cpu",
542	            dtype=dtype,
543	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
544	        )
545	        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
546	            top_ps,
547	            device="cpu",
548	            dtype=dtype,
549	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
550	        )
551	        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
552	            min_ps,
553	            device="cpu",
554	            dtype=dtype,
555	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
556	        )
557	        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
558	            presence_penalties,
559	            device="cpu",
560	            dtype=dtype,
561	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
562	        )
563	        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],

        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:

                prompt_tokens,

        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,

    def _init_sampling_tensors(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.
        """
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

        # Initialize new sampling tensors
        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
        """
        Single-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor

        Multi-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
            tensor
            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
            in the
            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
            structure

        Args:
            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
        """
        assert logits is not None
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
        elif self._do_penalties:
            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
            # between decode runs.
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)

        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve

def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import LogitsProcessorManager


@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:

    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool

    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]

    # None means no logprobs, 0 means sampled token logprobs only
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]

    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor

    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]

    # `allowed_token_ids_mask` is a 2D bool tensor of shape (max batch size,
    # vocab size).
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]

    # Loaded logits processors
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""

import torch
import torch.nn as nn

from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5


class Sampler(nn.Module):

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> SamplerOutput:
        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
        if num_logprobs is not None:
            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)

        # Use float32 for the logits.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
        # Apply allowed token ids.
        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
        # Apply bad words exclusion.
        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
            logits = processor.apply(logits)

        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
        # Sample the next token.
        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
        sampled = sampled.long()

        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)

        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)

        # These are GPU tensors.
        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
            # token per request.
            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
        )
        return sampler_output

    def apply_temperature(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        temp: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))

    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)

    def sample(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.

        The various logits processing functions called in this method
        may update the logits tensor in-place.
        """

        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
            greedy_sampled = None
        else:
            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
                return greedy_sampled

        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None

        # Apply temperature.
        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)

        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
        # (argmax invariant)
        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
            logits = processor.apply(logits)

        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.

# Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
        # until sequence is finished.
        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1

        self._verify_args()

        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
            self.top_p = 1.0
            self.top_k = 0
            self.min_p = 0.0
            self._verify_greedy_sampling()

        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)

    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
        if self.n < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
        if self.best_of is not None:
            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
            if self.best_of < 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
        if self.top_k < -1:
            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
            raise TypeError(
                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                             f"{self.min_p}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
        if self.min_tokens < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.worker.tpu_input_batch import InputBatch

DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
    temperature=-1.0,
    min_p=0.0,
    # strictly disabled for now
    top_k=0,
    top_p=1.0,
    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
    # presence_penalties=0.0,
    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
)


@dataclass
class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
    temperature: torch.Tensor = None

    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
    top_p: torch.Tensor = None

    all_greedy: bool = True

    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
    logprobs: bool = False

    # TODO No penalties for now
    no_penalties: bool = True
    prompt_token_ids = None
    frequency_penalties = None
    presence_penalties = None
    repetition_penalties = None
    # should use tensor
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())

    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized

    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
        default_factory=lambda: list())

    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
    bad_words_token_ids = None

    # Generator not supported by xla
    _generators: dict[int,
                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())

    @property
    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
        return self._generators

    @classmethod
    def from_input_batch(
        cls,
        input_batch: InputBatch,
        padded_num_reqs: int,
        xla_device: torch.device,
        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
        """
        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.

        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.

        Args:
            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
            xla_device: The XLA device.
            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
        """
        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)

        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs

        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
            # Pad value is the default one.
            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val

        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])

        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
        return cls(
            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
            to(xla_device),
            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            logprobs=needs_logprobs)
